title: SHIITAKE
description: Shiitake (Lentinula edodes) is an edible and medicinal mushroom rich in bioactive compounds including beta-glucans, polysaccharides, eritadenine, and antioxidants. It works through multiple mechanisms including immune modulation, cholesterol metabolism regulation, and antioxidant activity. Primarily used as a functional food and dietary supplement for immune support, cholesterol management, and overall health promotion.

research.title: Consuming Lentinula edodes (Shiitake) Mushrooms Daily Improves Human Immunity: A Randomized Dietary Intervention in Healthy Young Adults
research.summary: Daily consumption of shiitake mushrooms in healthy young adults resulted in improved immune function and reduced inflammatory markers, demonstrating immunomodulatory benefits in humans.

research.title: Therapeutic values and nutraceutical properties of shiitake mushroom (Lentinula edodes): A review
research.summary: Comprehensive review documenting shiitake's antimicrobial, antiviral, anticancer, anti-obesity, antidiabetic, and antioxidant properties, highlighting its potential for disease prevention and treatment through multiple bioactive compounds.

research.title: Anti-Cancer Potential of Edible/Medicinal Mushrooms in Breast Cancer
research.summary: Research demonstrates that shiitake mushrooms exhibit in vitro and in vivo anticancer effects against various tumors including breast cancer through multiple bioactive compounds and mechanisms.

research.title: Effects of Shiitake Culinary-Medicinal Mushroom, Lentinus edodes (Agaricomycetes), Bars on Lipid and Antioxidant Profiles in Individuals with Borderline High Cholesterol
research.summary: Double-blind randomized trial showed shiitake supplementation improved cholesterol profiles and antioxidant status in individuals with borderline high cholesterol.

research.title: Shiitake mushroom powder supplementation increase antioxidative activity in dogs
research.summary: Four weeks of shiitake supplementation at 800 mg/kg body weight significantly increased superoxide dismutase activity, decreased cholesterol, and enhanced sirtuin1 expression, demonstrating antioxidant benefits.

research.title: Shiitake Culinary-Medicinal Mushroom, Lentinus edodes (Agaricomycetes), Supplementation Alters Gut Microbiome and Corrects Dyslipidemia in Rats
research.summary: Shiitake supplementation modulated gut microbiome composition and corrected dyslipidemia in hypercholesterolemic rats, suggesting benefits through gut microbiota-mediated mechanisms.

research.title: Chemical Composition, Antioxidant and Anti-Inflammatory Activity of Shiitake Mushrooms (Lentinus edodes)
research.summary: Analysis of shiitake's chemical composition confirmed significant antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties attributed to its bioactive compounds including polysaccharides and phenolics.

research.title: Review of Bioactive Molecules Production, Biomass, and Basidiomata of Shiitake Culinary-Medicinal Mushrooms, Lentinus edodes (Agaricomycetes)
research.summary: Comprehensive review of shiitake's bioactive compounds including lentinan, polysaccharides, and secondary metabolites, demonstrating antibacterial, antifungal, anticancer, and immunomodulatory activities.

sentiment.summary: Users generally view shiitake mushrooms positively as part of comprehensive supplement stacks for immune support and longevity, though mostly mentioned alongside other medicinal mushrooms rather than as a standalone supplement. Quality and sourcing concerns are occasionally noted.

effects.label: Multi-Mushroom Formulas:
effects.description: Users typically combine shiitake with other medicinal mushrooms (Lion's Mane, Reishi, Cordyceps) rather than using it alone

effects.label: Quality Variability:
effects.description: Beta-glucan content varies significantly between products; some mycelium-on-grain products contain high starch (72.5% alpha-glucan) with minimal active compounds

effects.label: Dosage Consistency:
effects.description: Research studies used 30-60g fresh mushroom or 800mg/kg body weight powder daily with measurable benefits within 4 weeks to 4 months

effects.label: Preventive Focus:
effects.description: Most evidence supports long-term preventive benefits for cardiovascular health, immune function, and longevity rather than acute therapeutic effects

dosage.label: Standard Supplementation:
dosage.description: 30-60 grams of fresh mushroom or powder daily appears most common in research studies

dosage.label: Extract Dosing:
dosage.description: When using concentrated extracts in multi-mushroom blends, users typically take standardized capsules once or twice daily

dosage.label: Duration:
dosage.description: Benefits observed with consistent use over 4 weeks to several months; users often include in long-term health protocols

dosage.label: Cycling:
dosage.description: Some users cycle mushroom supplements 5 days on/2 days off, though research doesn't clearly support need for cycling

sideEffects.label: Generally Well-Tolerated:
sideEffects.description: Research and user reports indicate minimal side effects at recommended doses

sideEffects.label: Arsenic Concerns:
sideEffects.description: Some studies detected inorganic arsenic in commercial shiitake products, raising quality control concerns

sideEffects.label: Digestive Effects:
sideEffects.description: Rare reports of mild digestive changes when starting supplementation

sideEffects.label: Drug Interactions:
sideEffects.description: Potential interactions with cholesterol medications due to lipid-lowering effects warrant monitoring

availability.label: Brand Quality Varies:
availability.description: Users recommend Host Defense, Genius Mushrooms, and other established brands; emphasis on third-party testing for beta-glucan content

availability.label: Mycelium vs Fruiting Body:
availability.description: Significant quality differences exist; some products contain mainly starch from grain substrate rather than active mushroom compounds

availability.label: Widely Available:
availability.description: Accessible through health food stores, online retailers, and as whole mushrooms in grocery stores for dietary incorporation
